title: Freshly Chewy Comforting French Oxtail Stir-fry
description: This dish features a fresh and chewy oxtail stir-fry with pickled vegetables for added flavor, serving up a comforting meal that's also pescatarian-friendly.

ingredients.name: oxtail
ingredients.notes: Should be cut into small pieces for easier cooking.

ingredients.name: pickled vegetables
ingredients.notes: Use a variety of pickles such as radish, carrot, and cucumber.

ingredients.name: oyster sauce
ingredients.notes: To enhance the dish's savory taste without using fish products.

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Regular soy sauce can be used instead of oyster sauce for non-pescatarian diets.

instructions: Prepare all ingredients and set aside. Heat a wok or large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add a small amount of oil to the heated pan, then add the minced garlic and sauté until fragrant but not browned.
Once the garlic is aromatic, add the oxtail pieces to the pan and cook for about 3-5 minutes per side or until they develop a golden crust. Keep an eye on them to prevent burning.
Deglaze the wok with a splash of water to stir up any stuck bits from the bottom of the pan, then add oyster sauce and soy sauce. Mix well so all ingredients are coated evenly.
Add pickled vegetables and give everything a good toss to combine flavors, ensuring they are evenly distributed throughout the dish.
Finally, sprinkle the chopped scallions over the top for added color and freshness. Serve immediately while still warm.
